scenario_brief:
Test Scenario: Multiple Connections Per Direction

This scenario tests multiple room connections in the same direction, demonstrating the grid system's ability to position and align doors for side-by-side rooms.

Layout:
   [Server1][Server2][Server3]
           ↑    ↑    ↑
       [---- Hub ----]
              ↑
         [Reception]

Tests:
- Multiple connections in one direction (Hub has 3 north connections)
- Door spacing across wide rooms
- Alignment of multiple doors
- Array-based connection syntax

objects.name: Facility Access
objects.text:
Multiple Connection Test

The server hub to the north connects to three server rooms.
This tests the system's ability to handle multiple doors on one wall.

All server keys are in the hub.

objects.name: Hub Control
objects.text:
Server Hub - Central Control

This room has THREE north connections:
- Server Room 1 (Northwest)
- Server Room 2 (North)
- Server Room 3 (Northeast)

The doors should be evenly spaced along the north wall.

objects.name: Server 1 Log
objects.text:
Server Room 1 - West Position

This is the leftmost server room.
Door should align with the hub's northwest door.

objects.name: Server 2 Log
objects.text:
Server Room 2 - Center Position

This is the middle server room.
Door should align with the hub's center north door.

objects.name: Mission Complete
objects.text:
Server Room 3 - East Position

This is the rightmost server room.
Door should align with the hub's northeast door.

CONGRATULATIONS!

Multiple connections test complete!

This scenario demonstrated:
- Multiple connections in same direction (3 north doors on hub)
- Proper door spacing and alignment
- Array syntax for connections
- Side-by-side room positioning
- Asymmetric connection handling (hub: 3 south, each server: 1 north)
